---
title: "Course PM — Mechanics of Materials (MECH201)"
canvas:
  type: study_guide
  preprocess: true
  published: true
  front_page: true
  pdf:
    target_module: "Course Documents"
    filename: "CoursePM-MECH201.pdf"
    title: "Course PM (PDF)"
    published: true
---

# Introduction

Welcome to **Mechanics of Materials (MECH201)**, a 7.5 ECTS course covering the
mechanics of deformable solids: axial loading, torsion, bending, and combined
stresses.

# Schedule

| Week | Topic | Activity |
|:-----|:------|:---------|
| 1 | Stress & Strain | Lecture, Tensile Lab |
| 2 | Statics & Trusses | Lecture, Truss assignment |
| 3 | Beam Bending | Lecture, Concept quiz |
| 4 | Combined Loading | Project work |

# Examination

| Component | Credits | Grade |
|:----------|:--------|:------|
| Written exam | 4.5 ECTS | 5/4/3/F |
| Laboratory work | 1.5 ECTS | P/F |
| Design project | 1.5 ECTS | 5/4/3/F |

# Grading Criteria

| ILO | Fail | 3 | 4 | 5 |
|:----|:-----|:--|:--|:--|
| Stress analysis | Cannot set up equilibrium | Solves determinate cases | Handles indeterminate cases | Optimises designs |
| Material behaviour | Confuses stress and strain | States Hooke's law | Relates curves to failure | Predicts failure modes |
| Communication | No clear report | Readable report | Well-structured report | Publication quality |

# Key Formulae

The course revolves around a handful of relationships:

$$
\sigma = \frac{F}{A}, \qquad \tau = \frac{T r}{J}, \qquad \sigma = \frac{M c}{I}.
$$

# Teaching Staff

| Name | Role | Image | Link |
|:-----|:-----|:------|:-----|
| Dr. Test Lecturer | Course responsible | placeholder.png | https://example.com/staff |

# Research Connection

Course content connects to ongoing research in lightweight structures and
fatigue, as described in the education plan.
